zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp於1417年9月16日出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素時，她的父親William de Beauchamp62歲，母親Lady Catherine Ufflete48歲。她和Thomas Swynford至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1467年6月14日在英國倫敦享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp于1417年9月16日出生在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素时，她的父亲William de Beauchamp62岁，母亲Lady Catherine Ufflete48岁。她和Thomas Swynford至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1467年6月14日在英国倫敦享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp was born on 16 September 1417, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, her father, William de Beauchamp, was 62 and her mother, Lady Catherine Ufflete, was 48. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Thomas Swynford. She died on 14 June 1467, in London, England, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp est née le 16 septembre 1417, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 62 ans et sa mère, Lady Catherine Ufflete, avait 48 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Thomas Swynford. Elle est décédée le 14 juin 1467, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp am 16. September 1417 in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Beauchamp 62 und ihre Mutter Lady Catherine Ufflete 48. Sie hatte mit Thomas Swynford mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 14. Juni 1467 im Alter von 49 Jahren in London, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp è nata il il 16 settembre 1417 nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 62 anni e sua madre, Lady Catherine Ufflete, aveva 48 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Thomas Swynford. Morì il 14 giugno 1467, nel London, England, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Beauchampが1417年9月16日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで生まれた時，父William de Beauchampは62歳，母Lady Catherine Uffleteは48歳であった。彼女はThomas Swynfordとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1467年6月14日にイギリスロンドンで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp(이)가 1417년 9월 16일 영국 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 62세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Catherine Ufflete(은)는 48세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Swynford(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 6월 14일 영국 런던에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Beauchamp родилась 16 сентября 1417 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 62 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Catherine Ufflete, 48. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Thomas Swynford. Она умерла 14 июня 1467 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 49 лет.
